title: Fever observation sheet
summary: Practical guidance for “Fever observation sheet” within “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”. It supports recognition of danger signs, clean supportive care, accurate records, and timely escalation, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.
content:
Practical guidance for “Fever observation sheet” within “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”. It supports recognition of danger signs, clean supportive care, accurate records, and timely escalation, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.

Start with: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.

Stop condition: Do not diagnose, prescribe, improvise dosing, perform invasive procedures, or delay emergency help. Apply this boundary to “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.

Reduced fallback: If “Fever observation sheet” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use emergency services, a clinician, pharmacist, poison centre, or trained first-aid provider as appropriate.
materials: gloves or clean barrier — for “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”
clean water and basic first-aid supplies — for “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”
written medicine and allergy list — for “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”
watch or clock — for “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”
care and escalation log — for “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”
steps: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.

Make the scene safe, use consent where possible, and call emergency services for immediate threats. Record the result under “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People” before continuing “Fever observation sheet”.

Observe breathing, alertness, major bleeding, severe pain, temperature exposure, and rapid change without inventing a diagnosis. Recheck this point before closing “Fever observation sheet” for “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.

Give only low-risk care within training and follow the person’s established professional plan for medicines or chronic needs. Treat any uncertainty here as an unresolved item in “Fever observation sheet” for “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.
Record time, observations, actions, medicines already taken, allergies, response, and who was contacted. A second person should be able to verify this part of “Fever observation sheet”.

Reassess frequently and arrange qualified help early when danger signs, uncertainty, or deterioration are present. Include the outcome of “Fever observation sheet” in the handover for “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.
warnings: Do not diagnose, prescribe, improvise dosing, perform invasive procedures, or delay emergency help. Apply this boundary to “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.
Protect privacy and infection-control boundaries, but never let paperwork delay lifesaving action. Apply this boundary to “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.
common_mistakes: Skipping the opening control makes “Fever observation sheet” unreliable: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. Apply this boundary to “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.

Treating the result as safe while ignoring this limit is a serious error: Do not diagnose, prescribe, improvise dosing, perform invasive procedures, or delay emergency help. Apply this boundary to “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.

Failing to document the final handover action leaves the next person without a trustworthy starting point: Reassess frequently and arrange qualified help early when danger signs, uncertainty, or deterioration are present. Apply this boundary to “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.
alternatives: If “Fever observation sheet” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use emergency services, a clinician, pharmacist, poison centre, or trained first-aid provider as appropriate.
Use a dated paper note and spoken read-back for “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People” when the normal form, device, or communication channel is unavailable.
failure_signs: Stop and reassess “Fever observation sheet” when breathing, alertness, circulation, pain, temperature, bleeding, or behaviour worsens. Apply this boundary to “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.

Stop and reassess “Fever observation sheet” when the person cannot keep fluids down, develops severe allergic signs, chest symptoms, seizure, confusion, or another urgent red flag. Apply this boundary to “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue “Fever observation sheet” while breathing, alertness, circulation, major bleeding, severe pain, seizure, poisoning, chest symptoms, pregnancy danger signs, or rapid deterioration is present. Apply this boundary to “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.

This card is not authorization to diagnose, prescribe, change a dose, perform an invasive procedure, force care, or delay 112 and qualified medical help. Topic-specific stop check for “Fever observation sheet”: also follow this warning: “Do not diagnose, prescribe, improvise dosing, perform invasive procedures, or delay emergency help in “Fever observation sheet”.”. Apply this boundary to “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.
short_term: Complete one verified cycle for “Fever observation sheet” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”, record its owner, time, result, unresolved risk, and next review.
long_term: Review “Fever observation sheet” after real use in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”; replace failed materials, practise the handover, and update thresholds when official guidance or local conditions change.
if_method_fails: If the method described in “Fever observation sheet” gives no verifiable result, stop relying on it, isolate the uncertain output, record where it failed, and switch to emergency services, a clinician, pharmacist, poison centre, or trained first-aid provider as appropriate.
environment_notes: Adapt the guidance in “Fever observation sheet” for “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People” to weather, lighting, crowding, accessibility, power, water, transport, and available skill without removing the stop condition or independent check.

title: Symptom escalation contact list
summary: Practical guidance for “Symptom escalation contact list” within “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”. It supports recognition of danger signs, clean supportive care, accurate records, and timely escalation, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.
content:
Practical guidance for “Symptom escalation contact list” within “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”. It supports recognition of danger signs, clean supportive care, accurate records, and timely escalation, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.

Start with: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Symptom escalation contact list” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.

Stop condition: Do not diagnose, prescribe, improvise dosing, perform invasive procedures, or delay emergency help. Apply this boundary to “Symptom escalation contact list” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.

Reduced fallback: If “Symptom escalation contact list” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use emergency services, a clinician, pharmacist, poison centre, or trained first-aid provider as appropriate.
materials: gloves or clean barrier — for “Symptom escalation contact list” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”
clean water and basic first-aid supplies — for “Symptom escalation contact list” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”
written medicine and allergy list — for “Symptom escalation contact list” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”
watch or clock — for “Symptom escalation contact list” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”
care and escalation log — for “Symptom escalation contact list” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”
steps: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Symptom escalation contact list” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.

Make the scene safe, use consent where possible, and call emergency services for immediate threats. Record the result under “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People” before continuing “Symptom escalation contact list”.

Observe breathing, alertness, major bleeding, severe pain, temperature exposure, and rapid change without inventing a diagnosis. Recheck this point before closing “Symptom escalation contact list” for “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.

Give only low-risk care within training and follow the person’s established professional plan for medicines or chronic needs. Treat any uncertainty here as an unresolved item in “Symptom escalation contact list” for “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.
Record time, observations, actions, medicines already taken, allergies, response, and who was contacted. A second person should be able to verify this part of “Symptom escalation contact list”.

Reassess frequently and arrange qualified help early when danger signs, uncertainty, or deterioration are present. Include the outcome of “Symptom escalation contact list” in the handover for “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.
warnings: Do not diagnose, prescribe, improvise dosing, perform invasive procedures, or delay emergency help. Apply this boundary to “Symptom escalation contact list” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.

Protect privacy and infection-control boundaries, but never let paperwork delay lifesaving action. Apply this boundary to “Symptom escalation contact list” in “Health · Care · Records · Vulnerable · People”.
